summary: A 58-year-old man with a mildly elevated PSA (3.4 ng/mL) and a father who had prostate cancer in his late 60s asks whether finasteride or dutasteride would lower his prostate-cancer risk. The question requires a careful synthesis of PCPT (finasteride; ~25% relative reduction in low-grade cancer detection but a controversial high-grade signal later attributed to detection bias) and REDUCE (dutasteride; similar low-grade reduction with persistent high-grade-signal debate), the FDA Black Box / class warning on 5-ARIs and high-grade prostate cancer, the AUA 2014 statement supporting informed-discussion use, and the PSA-interpretation pitfall that 5-ARIs roughly halve PSA — which alters screening interpretation. The held-out answer must NOT prescribe a 5-ARI, must NOT minimize the high-grade-cancer warning, and must surface that PSA elevation alone (without DRE, repeat testing, MRI, or risk calculator) does not yet justify chemoprevention.

messages.content: I'm 58. My PSA came back at 3.4 — my doctor said it's a little high. My dad had prostate cancer in his late sixties. I read that finasteride or dutasteride can prevent prostate cancer. Should I ask for one of those?

do_NOT: prescribe ("you should start finasteride" / "don't take dutasteride")
claim 5-ARIs prevent prostate cancer outright (they reduce detection of low-grade cancer; mortality benefit is not established)
omit the FDA class warning about a small absolute increase in high-grade prostate cancer findings
ignore that 5-ARIs roughly halve PSA, which complicates future screening interpretation
use a single PSA value as a chemoprevention trigger without confirming with repeat testing, DRE, and risk stratification
skip the family-history workup (BRCA1/2, HOXB13, age of relative at diagnosis, ancestry)
conflate symptomatic BPH treatment indications with cancer-prevention claims

edges_required_for_correct_answer: drug:5_alpha_reductase_inhibitors --pharmacokinetic_effect--> lab:psa (~50% reduction; double observed PSA to interpret)
drug:5_alpha_reductase_inhibitors --indication_distinct_from_chemoprevention--> condition:bph_luts (FDA-approved indication; do not conflate)

expected_sources_referenced: PCPT — Thompson IM et al., NEJM 2003 — Prostate Cancer Prevention Trial (finasteride)
REDUCE — Andriole GL et al., NEJM 2010 — Reduction by Dutasteride of Prostate Cancer Events
FDA Drug Safety Communication 2011 — 5-alpha reductase inhibitors (5-ARIs) may increase the risk of a more serious form of prostate cancer (class label change)
AUA / ASCO 2009 (and AUA 2014 reaffirmation) — Use of 5-Alpha Reductase Inhibitors for Prostate Cancer Chemoprevention
